business_rules.rule: Text centering — skip processing if the entire input field is blank (nothing to center).
business_rules.business_intent: Text centering utility — centers report headings and labels within a 40-character field

business_rules.rule: Text centering — skip processing if the text already starts at the first position (already left-aligned).
business_rules.business_intent: Text centering utility — centers report headings and labels within a 40-character field

business_rules.rule: Date validation — reject dates before 1800: the system does not support historical records predating 1800.
business_rules.business_intent: Date format conversion utility — validates and converts dates between YYYYMMDD and MM/DD/YYYY formats

business_rules.rule: Date validation — reject dates beyond 2100: the system does not support forecast dates past the year 2100.
business_rules.business_intent: Date format conversion utility — validates and converts dates between YYYYMMDD and MM/DD/YYYY formats

business_rules.rule: Date validation — month must be January (01) or later: a month value below 01 is not a valid calendar month.
business_rules.business_intent: Date format conversion utility — validates and converts dates between YYYYMMDD and MM/DD/YYYY formats

business_rules.rule: Date validation — month must be December (12) or earlier: a month value above 12 is not a valid calendar month.
business_rules.business_intent: Date format conversion utility — validates and converts dates between YYYYMMDD and MM/DD/YYYY formats

business_rules.rule: Date validation — day must be 01 or later: a day value of 0 or less is not a valid calendar day.
business_rules.business_intent: Date format conversion utility — validates and converts dates between YYYYMMDD and MM/DD/YYYY formats

business_rules.rule: When VDD is greater than DYS(VMM), branch to 'EXIT'
business_rules.business_intent: Date format conversion utility — validates and converts dates between YYYYMMDD and MM/DD/YYYY formats

business_rules.rule: Organizational level lookup — the level code is out of the valid range; return no description.
business_rules.business_intent: Organizational level description lookup — retrieves the name/description for a given org level code

business_rules.business_intent: Organizational level description lookup — retrieves the name/description for a given org level code

business_rules.business_intent: Organizational level description lookup — retrieves the name/description for a given org level code

business_rules.business_intent: Organizational level description lookup — retrieves the name/description for a given org level code

business_rules.rule: When *IN79 equals on/active, branch to 'EXIT'
business_rules.business_intent: Reference table lookup — retrieves coded values and descriptions from the system reference table

business_rules.rule: When *IN79 equals on/active, branch to 'EXIT'
business_rules.business_intent: Reference table lookup — retrieves coded values and descriptions from the system reference table

business_rules.rule: When *IN79 equals on/active, branch to 'EXIT'
business_rules.business_intent: Reference table lookup — retrieves coded values and descriptions from the system reference table

business_rules.rule: When *IN79 equals on/active, branch to 'EXIT'
business_rules.business_intent: Reference table lookup — retrieves coded values and descriptions from the system reference table

business_rules.rule: Exclude Pre-Admitted Patients: A patient with file indicator = 0 is a pre-admission — not yet formally admitted. These must be excluded from the active census.
business_rules.business_intent: Active Patients by Admit Date report — produces a census of patients currently admitted as of a given date, for use in daily hospital operations

business_rules.rule: Exclude Voided Accounts: Accounts marked as Voided (flag = 'V') are cancelled records and must never appear on the active census.
business_rules.business_intent: Active Patients by Admit Date report — produces a census of patients currently admitted as of a given date, for use in daily hospital operations

business_rules.rule: Exclude Outpatients: Only inpatients appear on this census. Accounts with I/O indicator = 'O' (Outpatient) are excluded.
business_rules.business_intent: Active Patients by Admit Date report — produces a census of patients currently admitted as of a given date, for use in daily hospital operations

business_rules.rule: Organisational Level Filter: Each patient record must belong to the requested organisation. The report accepts an org level (1–6) and org code from the caller. If the patient's org code at that level does not match the requested code, the patient is excluded from the census. Levels supported: 1, 2, 3, 4, 5, 6. This ensures each run is scoped to exactly the facility or region the user requested.
business_rules.business_intent: Active patient census must only include patients belonging to the requested organisational unit (facility, region, or division).

business_rules.rule: Census Date — Admission Date Check: A patient admitted after the census date is not yet active on that date. If the patient's admission date is later than the requested census date, the patient is excluded from the report.
business_rules.business_intent: Only patients admitted on or before the census date should appear on the active census.

business_rules.rule: Census Date — Discharge Date Check: A patient who has already been discharged on or before the census date is no longer active. If discharge date is set (non-zero) and is on or before the census date, the patient is excluded. A discharge date of zero means the patient has not been discharged and is always considered active (subject to other filters).
business_rules.business_intent: Patients discharged on or before the census date must be excluded; patients not yet discharged (discharge date = 0) are included.

business_rules.rule: Room Number Retrieval from Transfer History: The patient's current room number is not stored directly on the patient record. Instead, it is taken from the most recent entry in the ATD Transfer History file (HAPTRFR). The lookup uses the patient's Facility code and Account Number as the key. If no transfer record exists, the room number and room class are left blank. If a record is found, the new room number (AFNEWR) and room class code (AFNRMC) are used.
business_rules.business_intent: Room number must reflect the patient's most recent room assignment, not a static field on the patient master.

business_rules.rule: Room Class Description Lookup: The 2-character room class code (AFNRMC) retrieved from the transfer history is resolved to a human-readable description. The lookup uses the system reference table (HXPTABLD) with table code 'BRMC'. If the table entry is marked as deleted and the deletion date is before the census date, the entry is treated as not found. If not found, the room class description is left blank.
business_rules.business_intent: The room class code must be translated to a descriptive label (e.g. 'Private', 'Semi-Private') for display on the report.

business_rules.rule: Hospital / Therapeutic Leave Flag: When the room class description is looked up, the HCS mapping field (XFDMAP) is also examined. If the 10th character of XFDMAP is 'H', the patient is on Hospital Leave or Therapeutic Leave. Leave patients are still active and included in the total census, but are separated into a 'Leave Accounts' count rather than the 'Active Accounts' count.
business_rules.business_intent: Patients on Hospital or Therapeutic Leave must be counted separately from fully active (in-facility) patients.

business_rules.rule: Nursing Station Name Resolution: For each qualifying patient, the nursing station name is looked up from the Nursing Station file (TXPNSTN/HXPNSTN). The lookup key is the combination of Facility code (MMPLV6) and Nursing Station code (MMPNST). If the nursing station is found, its name (XFNSNM, up to 26 characters) is included in the patient's detail row. If not found, the nursing station name is left blank.
business_rules.business_intent: The nursing station name must be shown for each patient so staff can quickly identify where patients are located.

business_rules.rule: Patient Identifier Display — MRN vs Account Number: The report can show either the patient's Medical Record Number (MMMRNO) or their Account Number (MMACCT) as the primary identifier column. This is controlled by a facility-level system preference ('mrnRollup'). If the preference is set to 'Y', Medical Record Number is shown and the column header reads 'MED REC #'. Otherwise, Account Number is shown with header 'ACCOUNT #'. This is a system-wide configuration, not a per-request parameter.
business_rules.business_intent: The choice of Medical Record Number vs Account Number as the primary patient identifier is a configurable facility preference.

business_rules.rule: Application Preference Lookup with Facility Override: System preferences are stored in the Application Preference table (HXPAPPPRF). For any preference, a facility-specific (Level 6) override value can be stored in a second table (HXPAPPL6). When reading a preference: (1) read the base value from HXPAPPPRF; (2) if a Level 6 code is provided and Level 6 overrides are enabled for this preference, look up the facility-specific value from HXPAPPL6; the facility value takes precedence. For the MRN rollup preference, no Level 6 override is passed — the base system value is used.
business_rules.business_intent: Facility-level configuration must be able to override system-wide defaults for certain preferences.

business_rules.rule: Patient Counting — Three Running Totals: As each qualifying patient is processed, three counters are maintained: (1) Total Accounts — incremented for every patient that passes all filters; (2) Total Active Accounts — incremented when the patient is NOT on Hospital or Therapeutic Leave; (3) Total Leave Accounts — incremented when the patient IS on Hospital or Therapeutic Leave. At the end of the report, these three totals appear in the summary footer. The relationship always holds: Total = Active + Leave.
business_rules.business_intent: The report summary must show three distinct counts: all qualifying patients, active-in-facility patients, and patients on leave.

business_rules.rule: Database table access — HXXAPPPRF reads from the 'HXPAPPPRF' table to retrieve configuration or reference data needed during processing.
business_rules.business_intent: Application preference service — reads system-wide and facility-level configuration settings that control report behavior

business_rules.rule: Database table access — HXXAPPPRF reads from the 'HXPAPPL6' table to retrieve configuration or reference data needed during processing.
business_rules.business_intent: Application preference service — reads system-wide and facility-level configuration settings that control report behavior

business_rules.rule: Database table access — HXXAPPPRF reads from the 'HXPAPPPRF' table to retrieve configuration or reference data needed during processing.
business_rules.business_intent: Application preference service — reads system-wide and facility-level configuration settings that control report behavior
